title: Electricians and electrical fitters
description: Research, design, develop, test, or supervise the manufacturing and installation of electrical equipment, components, or systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. All artists and related workers not listed separately. Play prerecorded music for live audiences at venues or events such as clubs, parties, or wedding receptions. May use techniques such as mixing, cutting, or sampling to manipulate recordings. May also perform as emcee (master of ceremonies). (Based on 27 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct or train workers to install, maintain, or repair electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures. Maintain current electrician's license or identification card to meet governmental regulations. Inspect electrical systems, equipment, or components to identify hazards, defects, or the need for adjustment or repair, and to ensure compliance with codes. Advise management on whether continued operation of equipment could be hazardous. Inspect and test equipment to locate damage or worn parts and diagnose malfunctions, or read work orders or schematic drawings to determine required repairs.

Assemble, install, test, or maintain electrical or electronic wiring, equipment, appliances, apparatus, or fixtures, using hand tools or power tools. Connect wires to circuit breakers, transformers, or other components. Test electrical systems or continuity of circuits in electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures, using testing devices, such as ohmmeters, voltmeters, or oscilloscopes, to ensure compatibility and safety of system. Diagnose malfunctioning systems, apparatus, or components, using test equipment and hand tools to locate the cause of a breakdown and correct the problem. Repair or replace wiring, equipment, or fixtures, using hand tools or power tools. Inspect electrical connections, wiring, relays, charging resistance boxes, and storage batteries, following wiring diagrams. Test and repair circuits and sensors, following wiring and system specifications. Disassemble defective equipment so that repairs can be made, using hand tools. Replace worn and defective parts such as switches, bearings, transmissions, belts, gears, circuit boards, or defective wiring. Reassemble units after repairs are made, making adjustments and cleaning and lubricating parts as needed.

Plan layout and installation of electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures, based on job specifications and local codes. Prepare sketches or follow blueprints to determine the location of wiring or equipment and ensure conformance to building and safety codes. Prepare technical drawings, specifications of electrical systems, or topographical maps to ensure that installation and operations conform to standards and customer requirements. Keep records of maintenance and repair work. Compile data and write reports regarding existing or potential electrical engineering studies or projects. Prepare specifications for purchases of materials or equipment. Keep informed of new products and developments. Order replacement parts.

Install, maintain, and repair electrical distribution and transmission systems, including conduits, cables, wires, and related equipment, such as transformers, circuit breakers, and switches. Lay underground cable directly in trenches, or string it through conduit running through the trenches. Climb poles or use truck-mounted buckets to access equipment. Drive vehicles equipped with tools and materials to job sites. String wire conductors and cables between poles, towers, trenches, pylons, and buildings, setting lines in place and using winches to adjust tension. Test conductors, according to electrical diagrams and specifications, to identify corresponding conductors and to prevent incorrect connections. Test equipment for overheating, using speed gauges and thermometers. Inspect completed installations and observe operations to ensure conformance to design and equipment specifications and compliance with operational, safety, or environmental standards. Inspect and test power lines and auxiliary equipment to locate and identify problems, using reading and testing instruments.

Use a variety of tools or equipment, such as power construction equipment, measuring devices, power tools, and testing equipment, such as oscilloscopes, ammeters, or test lamps. Work from ladders, scaffolds, or roofs to install, maintain, or repair electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures. Perform physically demanding tasks, such as digging trenches to lay conduit or moving or lifting heavy objects. Perform business management duties, such as maintaining records or files, preparing reports, or ordering supplies or equipment. Travel in trucks, helicopters, and airplanes to inspect lines for freedom from obstruction and adequacy of insulation. Demonstrate systems for customers and explain details, such as the causes and consequences of false alarms. Respond to emergency calls for problems such as gas leaks. Provide assistance during emergencies by operating floodlights or generators, placing flares, or driving needed vehicles.

Integrate electrical systems with renewable energy systems to improve overall efficiency. Develop systems that produce electricity with renewable energy sources, such as wind, solar, or biofuels. Design electrical systems or components that minimize electric energy requirements, such as lighting systems designed to account for natural lighting. Conduct field surveys or study maps, graphs, diagrams, or other data to identify and correct power system problems. Investigate or test vendors' or competitors' products. Assist in developing capital project programs for new equipment or major repairs. Oversee project production efforts to assure projects are completed on time and within budget. Supervise or train project team members as necessary. Confer with engineers, customers, or others to discuss existing or potential engineering projects or products.

Test battery charges, and replace or recharge batteries as necessary. Repair electric meters and components, such as transformers and relays, and replace metering devices, dial glasses, and faulty or incorrect wiring, using hand tools. Record maintenance information, including test results, material usage, and repairs made. Maintain stocks of parts. Install security systems, alarm devices, or related equipment, following blueprints of electrical layouts and building plans. Connect regulators to test stands, and turn screw adjustments until gauges indicate that inlet and outlet pressures meet specifications. Install regulators and related equipment such as gas meters, odorization units, and gas pressure telemetering equipment. Install gas pipes and water lines to connect appliances to existing gas lines or plumbing. Solder, wrap, and coat wires to ensure proper insulation.

Provide customers with cost estimates for equipment installation. Collect data relating to commercial or residential development, population, or power system interconnection to determine operating efficiency of electrical systems.

Provide electrical services in a safe and compliant manner, adhering to all safety practices and procedures, such as checking equipment regularly and erecting barriers around work areas. Report hazardous field situations and damaged or missing meters. Inspect installation sites and study work orders, building plans, and installation manuals to determine materials requirements and installation procedures.
Shut off service and notify repair crews when major repairs are required, such as the replacement of underground pipes or wiring.
Record maintenance and repair work performed on appliances, and record repairs required, parts used, and labor time.
Provide repair cost estimates, and recommend whether appliance repair or replacement is a better choice.
Maintain stocks of parts used in on-site installation, maintenance, and repair of appliances, and assemble new or reconditioned appliances.
Clean and reinstall parts, and clean, lubricate, and touch up minor defects on newly installed or repaired appliances.

Observe and examine appliances during operation to detect specific malfunctions such as loose parts or leaking fluid, and observe and test operation of appliances following installation, making necessary adjustments.
Refer to schematic drawings, product manuals, and troubleshooting guides to diagnose and repair problems, identifying methods for laying out and orienting installation.
Instruct customers regarding operation and care of appliances, providing emergency service numbers.
Level and connect appliances such as refrigerators and washing machines for proper operation, using hand tools.
Install appliances, such as refrigerators, washing machines, and stoves, and check they are functioning properly.
Measure velocity, horsepower, amperage, voltage, and other operational parameters of units or parts to diagnose problems using testing devices.
Contact supervisors or offices to receive repair assignments.
Light and adjust pilot lights on gas stoves and examine valves for gas leakage.
Investigate instances of illegal tapping into service lines, examine valves or mechanical control device parts for defects.
Install, inspect, and test electric meters, relays, and power sources to detect causes of malfunctions.
Test and repair track circuits, and inspect electrical units of railroad grade crossing gates, testing circuits and connections.
Inspect and maintain batteries, and inspect mechanisms using test equipment.
Perform routine maintenance on appliances and electrical equipment to keep them in operational condition.
Connect electrical wiring to control panels and electric motors.
Fabricate replacements for worn or broken parts using metalworking tools.
Connect hoses from provers to meter inlets and raise prover bells until prover gauges register zero.
Test gas pipelines and equipment to locate leaks and determine the pressure and flow of gas.
Compile or maintain records of system performance and maintenance, including paperwork such as work orders and service reports.
Join together components for installation of automated doors, elevators, and similar machinery, following blueprint specifications.
Install electrical wires and controls, ensuring proper connections and configurations in compliance with regulations.
Test and repair photovoltaic (PV) systems, ensuring proper functionality and safety measures are met, while conducting routine maintenance.
Identify and resolve deficiencies in the installation or materials of photovoltaic systems, ensuring code compliance.
Demonstrate the functionality and performance of installed systems to clients, ensuring expectations are met.
Examine and repair electrical and safety systems for a variety of equipment and vehicles, ensuring operational integrity.
Inspect and test operating conditions for elevators and lifts to ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Clean and perform minor repairs on installed machinery and systems as needed, maintaining safety and operational standards.
Acquire replacement parts and materials as necessary to maintain equipment and perform repairs.
Gather customer feedback and provide estimates for repair costs in a service-oriented environment.
Identify electrical hazards associated with installations and adhere to safety practices.
Ensure proper documentation and tracking of inventory and borrowed items for maintenance procedures.

Study blueprints, schematics, manuals, or other specifications to determine installation procedures and diagnose and repair problems with electrical systems and equipment, using visual inspections and testing instruments.

Inspect and test electrical systems and circuits for accurate assembly, installation, and to identify malfunctions or defects, using wiring diagrams and testing devices such as voltmeters or ammeters.

Repair, adjust, or replace defective electrical or mechanical components or parts, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering or welding equipment, and perform routine maintenance on electrical systems.
Install fixtures, outlets, terminal boards, switches, and wall boxes, using hand tools, and lay cable and connect electrical connections between machines, power sources, and other related equipment.
Maintain inventories of spare parts and order additional parts as necessary, and requisition materials and supplies needed for repairs or installations.
Calibrate testing instruments and equipment to prescribed specifications, and analyze equipment performance records to assess functioning and diagnose malfunctions.

Consult with customers to identify equipment problems and provide advice on operation, maintenance, or programming, as well as prepare cost estimates for repairs and conduct worker training in safety and operational procedures.
Document and maintain records detailing equipment tests, repairs, and maintenance operations, and prepare reports of findings related to completed work.
Operate and test equipment to ensure functionality, and demonstrate proper use of electrical systems and repair tools as necessary.
Coordinate efforts with other workers involved in installations or maintenance, and monitor work levels, progress, and safety conditions in the workplace.
